LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WITH OPPRESSION, FRAUD, OR MALICE. (SMITH V. SUP. CT. (1992) 10 CAL.APP.4TH 1033, 1042.) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-31-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows:
DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT is Granted with 30 days leave to amend. The complaint does not allege facts establishing that Defendant acted with oppression, fraud, or malice. (Smith v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1033, 1042.) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/ehg
